questions.question: What name, describing an ideally perfect place, actually means 'no place' in Greek?
What director's epic box-office bomb caused him to be dubbed 'the man who killed the western'?
What South African's real first name of Rolihlahla is translated as 'troublemaker'?
What word describes any grass that has seeds used for human or animal consumption?
What dreaded golf shot occurs when the ball is hit with the hosel of the club?
What White House worker did Dick Morris refer to as 'Stumpy,' according to Sherry Rowlands?
